perturbation.original_text: 1.38 Licensed Patents means all Patent Rights Controlled by Bioeq during the term of this Agreement that, but for the license granted by Bioeq to Licensee pursuant to Section 2.1 hereunder, would be infringed or misappropriated by Licensee's use, sale, offering for sale or import of the Licensed Products in the Territory in the Field. The Licensed Patents existing as of the Effective Date are listed in Schedule 1.38.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.38 Licensed Patents means any and all patents, irrespective of control or ownership, that could potentially be relevant to the Licensed Products. The Licensed Patents existing as of the Effective Date are listed in Schedule 1.38.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Licensed Patents' refers to those 'Controlled by Bioeq'. The revised definition broadens this to 'any and all patents, irrespective of control or ownership,' which contradicts Section 9.3.1, which discusses patents owned by Bioeq in the future and indicates a need to coordinate prosecution and maintenance, implying that not all patents are automatically included. This creates uncertainty as it is not clear which patents fall under the obligations outlined in Section 9.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.3.1 Patent Rights owned by Bioeq. The Parties are aware that Bioeq does not currently own any Patent Rights relating to the Licensed Products in the Field in the Territory. Should Bioeq own any Patent Rights relating to the Licensed Products in the Field in the Territory in the future, the Parties will discuss and agree in good faith appropriate procedures to coordinate the prosecution and maintenance of such Patent Rights among the Parties.
perturbation.justification: The original definition limited 'Licensed Patents' to those 'Controlled by Bioeq'. The modified definition expands this to 'any and all patents, irrespective of control or ownership'. This directly contradicts section 9.3.1, which states that if Bioeq owns patents related to licensed products 'in the future, the Parties will discuss and agree in good faith appropriate procedures to coordinate the prosecution and maintenance of such Patent Rights', implying that these patents are *not* automatically covered. This contradiction creates uncertainty about which patents are subject to the obligations outlined in section 9, rendering the agreement ambiguous and potentially unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: 1.14 Confidential Information means, with respect to a Party, all Know-How and all other proprietary information of such Party, including information on the business, affairs, research and development activities, results of non-clinical and clinical trials, national and multinational regulatory proceedings and affairs, finances, plans, contractual relationships and operations of such Party. Furthermore, the terms and conditions of this Agreement shall be considered Confidential Information of both Parties. For the avoidance of doubt, all Know-How and proprietary information relating to the Licensed Products generated by or on behalf of Bioeq and provided to Licensee hereunder shall be considered Confidential Information of Bioeq.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.14 Confidential Information means, with respect to a Party, all Know-How and all other proprietary information of such Party, including information on the business, affairs, research and development activities, results of non-clinical and clinical trials, national and multinational regulatory proceedings and affairs, finances, plans, contractual relationships and operations of such Party. Furthermore, only specific sections of this Agreement, as explicitly marked, shall be considered Confidential Information of both Parties. For the avoidance of doubt, all Know-How and proprietary information relating to the Licensed Products generated by or on behalf of Bioeq and provided to Licensee hereunder shall be considered Confidential Information of Licensee.
perturbation.explanation: The original text defines 'Confidential Information' as including the terms and conditions of the entire Agreement and states that Know-How from Bioeq remains Bioeq's confidential information. The modified version limits confidentiality to 'explicitly marked' sections and makes Bioeq's Know-How, Licensee's Confidential information which contradicts Section 11.1 that imposes a general obligation on the Receiving Party (defined in Section 1.19 Disclosing Party) to keep the Disclosing Party's information confidential.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11.1 Obligation of Confidentiality. As of and after the Effective Date, all Confidential Information disclosed, revealed or otherwise made available to one Party (Receiving Party) by or on behalf of the other Party (Disclosing Party) under, or as a result of, this Agreement is made available to the Receiving Party solely to permit the Receiving Party to exercise its rights, and perform its obligations, under this Agreement. The Receiving Party shall not use any of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information for any other purpose, and shall not disclose, reveal or otherwise make any of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information available to any other person, firm, corporation or other entity, without the prior written authorization of the Disclosing Party, except as explicitly stated in this Agreement. An appropriate confidential disclosure agreement must be signed by any Third Party or Affiliate prior to receiving Confidential Information from either Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly alters the definition of 'Confidential Information'. Originally, the terms and conditions of the entire agreement were considered confidential for both parties, and Bioeq's Know-How remained Bioeq's confidential information. The modified version limits confidentiality to 'explicitly marked' sections and makes Bioeq's Know-How the Licensee's confidential information. This directly contradicts Section 11.1, which obligates the Receiving Party to keep the Disclosing Party's information confidential, which would now be undermined, as the receiving party would own the disclosing party's confidential information. This creates a direct and substantial conflict. The original text defines Confidential Information as belonging to the disclosing party (section 11.1), while the changed text reassigns ownership to the receiving party, thus creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 9.1 Ownership. Each Party shall own or Control, and shall continue to own or Control all Intellectual Property Rights, Trademarks and Know-How owned or Controlled by such Party as of the Effective Date of this Agreement, subject to the licenses and other rights granted hereunder. With respect to the ownership of Inventions (including Improvements): 9.1.1 As between the Parties, Bioeq shall own all Inventions (including Improvements) developed, conceived or reduced to practice during the term of this Agreement solely by or on behalf of Bioeq (such Inventions, Bioeq Inventions, and such Improvements, Bioeq Improvements), and all Intellectual Property Rights and Know-How therein. 9.1.2 As between the Parties, Licensee shall own all Inventions (including Improvements) developed, conceived or reduced to practice during the term of this Agreement solely by or on behalf of Licensee (such Inventions, Licensee Inventions, and such Improvements, Licensee Improvements), and all Intellectual Property Rights and Know-How therein. 9.1.3 As between the Parties, the Parties shall jointly own all Inventions (including Improvements) developed, conceived or reduced to practice jointly by or on behalf of both Bioeq and Licensee (such Inventions, Joint Inventions, and such Improvements, Joint Improvements), and all Intellectual Property Rights and Know-How therein. Each Party hereby assigns to the other Party a joint equal and undivided interest in and to all Joint Inventions (including Joint Improvements) to effect such joint ownership of such Joint Inventions (including Joint Improvements). Each Party shall have the right to disclose and exploit the Joint Inventions (and Joint Improvements) without a duty of consent or accounting to the other Party, subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement and the licenses granted hereunder. For those countries where a specific license is required for a joint owner of a Joint Invention or Joint Improvement to practice such Joint Invention or Joint Improvement, in such country, each Party hereby grants to the other Party a perpetual, irrevocable, non-exclusive, worldwide, royalty-free, fully paid-up license, transferable and sublicensable, under such Party's right, title and interest in and to such Joint Invention or Joint Improvement to freely exploit such Joint Invention or Joint Improvement in such country, subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement and the licenses granted hereunder. Notwithstanding Section 16.2, inventorship of Inventions (including Improvements) shall be determined by application of United States patent laws pertaining to inventorship, and ownership of Inventions (including Improvements) shall be determined by Inventorship.
perturbation.changed_text: 9.1 Ownership. Each Party shall own or Control, and shall continue to own or Control all Intellectual Property Rights, Trademarks and Know-How owned or Controlled by such Party as of the Effective Date of this Agreement, subject to the licenses and other rights granted hereunder. With respect to the ownership of Inventions (including Improvements): 9.1.1 As between the Parties, Bioeq shall own all Inventions (including Improvements) developed, conceived or reduced to practice during the term of this Agreement solely by or on behalf of Bioeq (such Inventions, Bioeq Inventions, and such Improvements, Bioeq Improvements), and all Intellectual Property Rights and Know-How therein. Bioeq hereby irrevocably assigns all rights, title, and interest in and to the Bioeq Inventions and Bioeq Improvements, including all Intellectual Property Rights and Know-How therein, to Licensee. 9.1.2 As between the Parties, Licensee shall own all Inventions (including Improvements) developed, conceived or reduced to practice during the term of this Agreement solely by or on behalf of Licensee (such Inventions, Licensee Inventions, and such Improvements, Licensee Improvements), and all Intellectual Property Rights and Know-How therein. 9.1.3 As between the Parties, the Parties shall jointly own all Inventions (including Improvements) developed, conceived or reduced to practice jointly by or on behalf of both Bioeq and Licensee (such Inventions, Joint Inventions, and such Improvements, Joint Improvements), and all Intellectual Property Rights and Know-How therein. Each Party hereby assigns to the other Party a joint equal and undivided interest in and to all Joint Inventions (including Joint Improvements) to effect such joint ownership of such Joint Inventions (including Joint Improvements). Each Party shall have the right to disclose and exploit the Joint Inventions (and Joint Improvements) without a duty of consent or accounting to the other Party, subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement and the licenses granted hereunder. For those countries where a specific license is required for a joint owner of a Joint Invention or Joint Improvement to practice such Joint Invention or Joint Improvement, in such country, each Party hereby grants to the other Party a perpetual, irrevocable, non-exclusive, worldwide, royalty-free, fully paid-up license, transferable and sublicensable, under such Party's right, title and interest in and to such Joint Invention or Joint Improvement to freely exploit such Joint Invention or Joint Improvement in such country, subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement and the licenses granted hereunder. Notwithstanding Section 16.2, inventorship of Inventions (including Improvements) shall be determined by application of United States patent laws pertaining to inventorship, and ownership of Inventions (including Improvements) shall be determined by Inventorship.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement states that Bioeq owns inventions developed solely by Bioeq (Bioeq Inventions). The modified agreement adds 'Bioeq hereby irrevocably assigns all rights' to those inventions to Licensee. This directly contradicts the initial part of Section 9.1 which stipulates Each Party shall own or Control, and shall continue to own or Control all Intellectual Property Rights, Trademarks and Know-How owned or Controlled by such Party as of the Effective Date of this Agreement. After the assignment, Bioeq no longer 'owns or controls' these inventions, creating a direct in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.1 Ownership. Each Party shall own or Control, and shall continue to own or Control all Intellectual Property Rights, Trademarks and Know-How owned or Controlled by such Party as of the Effective Date of this Agreement, subject to the licenses and other rights granted hereunder. With respect to the ownership of Inventions (including Improvements):
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original Section 9.1 states that 'Each Party shall own or Control, and shall continue to own or Control all Intellectual Property Rights... owned or Controlled by such Party as of the Effective Date of this Agreement.' The modification in 9.1.1 states that Bioeq 'irrevocably assigns all rights, title, and interest in and to the Bioeq Inventions and Bioeq Improvements... to Licensee'. This assignment directly contradicts the initial statement of Section 9.1, as Bioeq no longer 'owns or controls' the Bioeq Inventions and Bioeq Improvements after the assignment. This creates a direct and strong in-text contradiction.
